title: Attributes, Filters, Merge Drivers, and Content Policy · Mastery Check
summary: A maintainer review compares near-miss operations for attributes, filters, merge drivers, and content policy and requires the smallest safe path-attribute and content-transformation policy with topology and content evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for path-attribute and content-transformation policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Resolve attributes from system, repository, info, directory, pattern, and negative states using check-attr evidence.
Design text, eol, working-tree-encoding, binary, and renormalization policy with byte-level and cross-platform verification.
Configure diff drivers, textconv, binary markers, merge drivers, recursive behavior, and conflict-marker size without treating presentation as storage.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Gita Attribute Precedence Query · changed case
steps.instruction:
quiz case — Case G32 is a quiz in a disposable repository. a directory-level attribute overrides expected line-ending and filter policy. The learner must predict the effective text, eol, filter, diff, and merge states are reported for the exact path, run git check-attr --all -- scripts/release.sh, explain release.sh is text with eol=crlf because a nearer attribute rule overrides root policy, test this changed condition: remove the required filter tool and require checkout to fail instead of silently losing data, and preserve this recovery boundary: record byte digests before renormalization and retain the pre-migration ref.

Which action demonstrates Gita Attribute Precedence Query without dropping earlier contracts?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for resolve attributes from system, repository, info, directory, pattern, and negative states using check-attr evidence.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write the quiz evidence note for Gita Large Content Policy: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Case G39 is a quiz in a disposable repository. a directory-level attribute overrides expected line-ending and filter policy. The learner must predict the effective text, eol, filter, diff, and merge states are reported for the exact path, run git check-attr --all -- scripts/release.sh, explain release.sh is text with eol=crlf because a nearer attribute rule overrides root policy, test this changed condition: remove the required filter tool and require checkout to fail instead of silently losing data, and preserve this recovery boundary: record byte digests before renormalization and retain the pre-migration ref.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Choose ordinary Git, LFS-style pointers, release artifacts, package registries, or external storage from review, locking, retention, availability, and migration needs.
